title: London, Manchester, Edinburgh, Inverness & Glasgow.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scheduled inter-city trains linking each stop, with private transfers on arrival and departure.

transport.1: London airport to London hotel private transfer.
transport.2: London Hop-on Hop-off 1-day pass with one-way Thames river cruise.
transport.4: London to Manchester train, standard class (approx. 2h 10m).
transport.6: Manchester to Edinburgh train, standard class (approx. 3h 30m).
transport.8: Edinburgh to Inverness train, standard class (approx. 3h 30m) through the Cairngorms.
transport.10: Inverness to Glasgow train, standard class (approx. 3h 15m).
transport.11: Glasgow hotel to Glasgow airport private transfer.

inclusions: London airport–hotel private transfer on arrival
London Hop-on Hop-off 1-day ticket with one-way Thames river cruise
London to Manchester train ticket, standard class
Manchester to Edinburgh train ticket, standard class

private.name: Private Exclusive Coach
private.blurb: A private vehicle and driver on disposal for the whole itinerary, with guided touring in London and the Highlands.

transport.1: London airport to London hotel private transfer. Private vehicle on disposal for the full itinerary.
transport.2: London city tour, 3 hrs with guide, plus London Eye standard tickets and a one-way Thames river cruise.
transport.3: Stonehenge & Bath excursion, with entrances, by private vehicle.
transport.8: Private transfer Edinburgh → Inverness, through the Cairngorms.
transport.10: Private transfer Inverness → Glasgow, via Loch Ness and Loch Lomond.
transport.11: Glasgow hotel to Glasgow airport private transfer.

inclusions: Private vehicle on disposal for the full itinerary
London airport–hotel private transfers on arrival and departure
London city tour, 3 hrs with guide
London Eye standard tickets and one-way Thames river cruise
Private transfers between London, Manchester, Edinburgh, Inverness and Glasgow

days.title: Arrive London
days.desc: Arrive in the capital and transfer to your hotel. The rest of the day is free to get your bearings — Westminster, the Thames embankment and Covent Garden are all within easy reach of the central hotels.
days.taste: A classic fish and chips supper at a traditional London pub is the perfect first-night welcome.

days.title: London
days.desc: A full day discovering London with the Hop-on Hop-off ticket, taking in Buckingham Palace, the Tower of London and St Paul's Cathedral, then a one-way cruise along the Thames.
days.experience: An evening in London's West End, home to some of the world's longest-running theatre productions.

days.title: Stonehenge & Bath excursion
days.desc: A day trip out of the city to Stonehenge, the prehistoric stone circle on Salisbury Plain, followed by the elegant Georgian city of Bath and its Roman-era baths.
days.taste: Sally Lunn's in Bath serves its famous buns from a house dating to 1482, a short walk from the Roman Baths.

days.title: London to Manchester
days.desc: Travel north to Manchester, a city shaped by the Industrial Revolution and reborn through music, football and a thriving arts scene around the Northern Quarter.

days.title: Manchester
days.desc: A day at leisure to explore Manchester: the Science and Industry Museum, the canalside warehouses of Castlefield, and the city's famous football heritage at Old Trafford.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Manchester United stadium tour available.

days.title: Manchester to Edinburgh
days.desc: Cross the border into Scotland and arrive in Edinburgh, its skyline dominated by the castle perched high on its volcanic rock above the Old Town.

days.title: Edinburgh
days.desc: A full day in Edinburgh: Edinburgh Castle, the Royal Mile with its closes and courtyards, and the view over the city from Arthur's Seat.
days.shopping: The Royal Mile and George Street are lined with tartan, tweed and single-malt whisky shops.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Edinburgh Castle & Royal Mile tour available.

days.title: Edinburgh to Inverness
days.desc: Head north through the Cairngorms to Inverness, the gateway to the Scottish Highlands on the banks of the River Ness.

days.title: Inverness & Loch Ness
days.desc: A day at leisure around Inverness — Inverness Castle, the riverside walks, and the legendary waters of Loch Ness just outside the city.
days.taste: A Speyside single-malt whisky tasting is a fitting way to sample the Highlands' best-known export.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Loch Ness cruise available.

days.title: Inverness to Glasgow
days.desc: Journey south via Loch Ness, Glencoe and Loch Lomond to Glasgow, Scotland's largest city, known for its Victorian architecture and Merchant City.

days.title: Depart Glasgow
days.desc: Free time until your private transfer to Glasgow airport, depending on flight time.

hotels.h3: Hilton Garden Inn Manchester Emirates Old Trafford
Holiday Inn Express Edinburgh City Centre
Holiday Inn Express Glasgow City Centre Riverside

about.title: Best season
about.body: May–September for the longest, mildest days; the Highlands are especially scenic in early autumn.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Temperate and changeable throughout, typically 12–20°C in summer, cooler and wetter further north in Scotland.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Pound sterling (£) throughout; the United Kingdom is outside the Schengen area, so standard UK entry requirements apply for the whole itinerary.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Book Edinburgh Castle and the Tower of London ahead in summer — both sell out days in advance. Arrive at Stonehenge early to avoid coach-tour crowds.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: Seat reservations are included on the inter-city services. Allow extra connection time at Manchester Piccadilly and Edinburgh Waverley.

goodToKnow.title: Your vehicle
goodToKnow.body: Driver working hours are limited by law, so long driving days (such as Inverness to Glasgow) include a scheduled rest stop. Luggage space fits one large case per guest.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Layers and a waterproof jacket year-round, especially north of Edinburgh. Comfortable, sturdy shoes for cobbled streets and hillside paths.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere across England and Scotland; a little cash is useful in smaller Highland towns. Tipping around 10% in restaurants is customary.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
